label: The Nesting Dolls
subject: Nesting dolls (no code)
bossHook: Open a nesting doll: each shell holds a smaller doll inside, until the tiny solid core that opens no further — then close them all back up.

vocab.stop: STOP  ·  if this doll is solid, keep it & close up
vocab.descend: OPEN  ·  take out the smaller doll inside
vocab.descendSame: OPEN  ·  put the SAME doll back  ⚠ not smaller

intro-return.title: Closing Them Back Up
intro-return.hook: Opening dolls is only half of it — once you reach the solid core, you close every shell back up in reverse. Open it and watch them close.
intro-return.winText: You reached the solid core, then closed every doll back up in reverse order. That closing-up is recursion 'unwinding'.

learn.frame: Opening a doll is the easy half — but where does the closing-up come from, and in what order?

reveal.body: Every shell you opened stayed in your hands, waiting. Reaching the solid core let them close one at a time — innermost first, outermost last. That reverse close-up is the call stack UNWINDING.
reveal.inTheWild: Each open doll is a paused call held in memory; they finish in the reverse of the order you opened them.

base-case.title: The Solid Core
base-case.hook: This keeps opening dolls forever — even the tiny solid ones. Add a STOP so that when you reach the SOLID core, you stop opening.
base-case.winText: The STOP recognised the solid core and stopped. That 'smallest, opens-no-further' doll is the BASE CASE.

failText.missing-base-case: No STOP — you kept trying to open dolls that were already solid, forever. Every nesting needs a smallest core to stop at.

reveal.body: The STOP marked the solid doll — the one you keep instead of opening. With no 'this opens no further', there's nothing to hand back. That solid doll is the BASE CASE.
reveal.inTheWild: the empty box, a single item, a tree leaf — the innermost thing you don't open.

insights.missing-base-case: The base case is the one doll you DON'T open. Without it every doll leads to another, forever — the pile of held-open shells never stops growing.

recursive-case.title: Smaller Each Time
recursive-case.hook: You'll stop at the solid core — but each step must take out a SMALLER doll, not the same one. Add an OPEN that reveals the smaller doll inside.
recursive-case.winText: Each OPEN revealed a smaller doll until the core. Going one size smaller each time is the RECURSIVE CASE.

failText.no-progress: Your OPEN put the SAME doll back, so it never got smaller and never reached the core. Each step must go one size down.

reveal.body: Each OPEN gave up a strictly SMALLER doll, so you edged toward the solid core. Making the thing one size smaller every step is the RECURSIVE CASE.
reveal.inTheWild: each call works on a smaller piece — a shorter list, a child folder, the doll one size down.

insights.no-progress: The core is reachable only if every open yields something smaller. Put the same doll back and you never approach it — the nesting can't resolve.

boss-descent.title: BOSS · The Grand Doll
boss-descent.hook: Nothing placed. Build it all: STOP at the solid core AND OPEN a smaller doll each time. Four dolls deep. Go.
boss-descent.winText: Base case + shrinking step, from scratch. Boss down — same shape as the wizard's spell, different subject.

failText.missing-base-case: No STOP — you opened forever. There is no smaller doll than the core.
failText.no-progress: It never went smaller — you opened the same doll again and again.

learn.frame: Nothing set. What two things must a doll have so it opens all the way in AND closes all the way back up?

reveal.body: A solid core to stop at, plus an OPEN that reveals something smaller each time — that pairing is the whole idea. Base case to end + recursive case that shrinks toward it IS recursion.
reveal.inTheWild: the same two parts everywhere: the piece you handle directly, and the piece you break into something smaller.

insights.missing-base-case: The core is what turns the process around; with no innermost stop there's nothing to close back up from, only endless opening.
insights.no-progress: A core you never reach is no help — the shrinking is what carries each open toward it; skip it and the base case stays out of reach.

bottomless-pit.title: SIDE-QUEST · Forever Opening
bottomless-pit.hook: You kept opening past the core. Drill ONE thing: place the STOP so you know when a doll is solid and quit.
bottomless-pit.winText: STOP placed — you recognise the solid core now. The base case is what ends the nesting.

failText.missing-base-case: Still no STOP — still opening forever. The core has to be checked BEFORE you open again.

reveal.body: One STOP that spots the solid doll, and the opening ends. The base case is simply asking 'is this one solid?' before you try to open again.
reveal.inTheWild: the check at the top of the routine: if it's already smallest, keep it and return.

insights.missing-base-case: The 'is it solid?' test comes FIRST, before any open — that's what lets the smallest doll end the nesting instead of being cracked open too.

heal-recursive-case.title: One Size Down
heal-recursive-case.hook: The solid core is your stopping point — that part's done. Each step now has to reveal a SMALLER doll, not set the same one back. Choose the OPEN that goes one size down.
heal-recursive-case.winText: Each OPEN gave up a smaller doll — size 2 → 1 → core — until it stopped. Going exactly one size smaller each time is the RECURSIVE CASE.

failText.no-progress: Your step kept opening the SAME-size doll, so it never got smaller and never reached the core — it just piled up forever. The recursive case must pass a smaller doll inward.

reveal.body: size 2 → 1 → core, because each OPEN gave up a doll one size smaller. That steady step-down toward the core is the RECURSIVE CASE.
reveal.inTheWild: always hand inward something smaller than you got — one size down, one item fewer.

insights.no-progress: There has to be some measure — here, the doll's size — that drops by at least one every open. That shrinking measure is what guarantees you reach the core.
